sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: TODO: Articulate why a visual dashboard is needed. Consider: developers and tech leads need a bird's-eye view of the codebase knowledge graph — staleness hotspots, test coverage gaps, and documentation health — that the CLI alone cannot convey quickly. The viz dashboard makes that information interactive and explorable.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
TODO: Write user stories. Suggestions:
- As a developer I want to see a graph of my codebase so I can understand module dependencies at a glance
- As a tech lead I want to filter by staleness status so I can triage documentation debt
- As a developer I want to click a node and see its docstring, history, and test results in a side panel
- As a developer I want to browse and edit DocJSON docs inline so I don't have to switch to a text editor
- As a CI engineer I want an API endpoint that returns staleness counts so I can build dashboard widgets

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.content:
Current implementation covers:
- Interactive Cytoscape.js graph with subtype-based coloring (was node_type-based) and staleness border rings
- Staleness rings use unified vocabulary: CONTENT_STALE, DESC_STALE, LINKED_STALE, STRUCTURAL_DRIFT, VERIFIED
- `/api/all` returns persisted staleness column directly — no history-table computation or background refresh needed
- `/api/check` recomputes staleness via shared engine, writes to DB, returns results
- `_refreshStaleness` fire-and-forget JS removed — staleness is always current from DB
- Filterable node list table with sortable columns
- Detail drawer showing node metadata, summary, docstring, dFlow steps, history, and test results
- DocJSON viewer/editor with section-level editing, link picker, Mermaid diagram editor
- Workflow & task explorer with step drill-down and Monaco source viewer
- Test explorer with JUnit integration, fixture linking, tier badges (T1/T2/T3)
- Focus mode: ego-network exploration from a seed node with configurable depth and direction
- Real-time rescan: POST /api/rescan re-indexes and refreshes
- Staleness check panel: GET /api/check returns per-node staleness with new vocabulary
- Config node rendering: `subtype=config` nodes from `.claude/` get distinct orange coloring in graph and subtype badge in list; markdown config files render with inline preview in list view

sections.heading: Non-Goals
sections.content:
TODO: Confirm non-goals. Likely candidates:
- Real-time collaboration (multi-user editing)
- Authentication or access control (local-only tool)
- Persistent server deployment (intended for `cortex viz` local sessions)
- Mobile-responsive layout

sections.heading: Icebox / Future Ideas
sections.content:
TODO: Capture stretch goals. Ideas from the codebase:
- Diff view: show code changes between history checkpoints
- Coverage heatmap overlay on the graph
- Export graph as SVG/PNG
- Keyboard shortcuts for node navigation
- Dark mode toggle
